blocks.body: Should / Ought to helps you give advice, make recommendations, and talk about expected results.

{"level":"A2-B1","intro":"In this topic you will see how should and ought to help you give advice, recommendations, and talk about what we expect as a normal result.","rules":[{"label":"ADVICE","color":"emerald","text":"Should is the most common form for advice or a gentle recommendation.","example":"You should get some rest."},{"label":"EXPECTATION","color":"blue","text":"Should also shows that something will probably happen or is the correct result in this situation.","example":"The train should arrive soon."},{"label":"OUGHT TO","color":"amber","text":"Ought to is very close to should, but can sound slightly more formal or moral.","example":"You ought to apologize."}]}

{"title":"1. Key forms","intro":"Should behaves like a modal verb. Ought to keeps the particle to.","items":[{"label":"Advice / opinion","title":"should + V","subtitle":"should study / shouldn't worry / Should I call?"},{"label":"Recommendation","title":"ought to + V","subtitle":"ought to rest / ought not to wait"},{"label":"Expectation","title":"should + V","subtitle":"should arrive / should be ready"},{"label":"Structure warning","title":"not: should to","subtitle":"should go, but ought to go"}]}

{"title":"2. Main uses","sections":[{"label":"Advice and recommendation","color":"emerald","description":"Most often, should and ought to help you say that something is a good idea or a sensible step.","examples":[{"en":"You should talk to your teacher.","ua":"You should talk to your teacher."},{"en":"We ought to leave earlier next time.","ua":"We ought to leave earlier next time."},{"en":"He shouldn't spend all his money at once.","ua":"He shouldn't spend all his money at once."}]},{"label":"Expected result","color":"blue","description":"Should often shows that something is expected to happen because of logic, plans, or experience.","examples":[{"en":"The package should arrive tomorrow.","ua":"The package should arrive tomorrow."},{"en":"This key should open the back door.","ua":"This key should open the back door."},{"en":"Everything should be easier after the update.","ua":"Everything should be easier after the update."}]},{"label":"Should or ought to?","color":"amber","description":"In most everyday situations, they are very close in meaning. Should sounds more usual, while ought to can sound slightly more formal or moral.","examples":[{"en":"You should call her.","ua":"You should call her."},{"en":"You ought to call her.","ua":"You ought to call her."},{"en":"People ought to respect the rules.","ua":"People ought to respect the rules."}],"note":"In everyday conversation, should is more common than ought to."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Adding to after should.","wrong":"You should to rest.","right":"✅ You should rest.","hint":"After should, use the bare infinitive."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Forgetting to after ought.","wrong":"We ought leave now.","right":"✅ We ought to leave now.","hint":"The full form is ought to + verb."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Adding an ending to the main verb.","wrong":"The train should arrives soon.","right":"✅ The train should arrive soon.","hint":"After should, the verb does not take -s."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Should = advice, recommendation, expected result.","Ought to is very close to should, but sounds slightly more formal or moral.","After should, use the base verb without to.","After ought to, the particle to stays: ought to check.","In ordinary speech, should is usually more common and more natural."]}
